game_name: Tiny Toon Adventures 2: Trouble in Wackyland

design_philosophy.core_loop: Select themed amusement park rides using different character abilities, accumulate tickets through point scoring, earn enough tickets to enter final Funhouse challenge
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management (tickets) combined with character-specific platforming challenges; rides are deceptively challenging despite whimsical theme
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Experience various amusement park attractions as beloved Tiny Toons characters, each with unique movement mechanics and abilities
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Multi-character progression system where each ride showcases a distinct character's ability rather than single-protagonist approach; ticket economy gates access to final content; refuses to soften difficulty despite cartoon IP and colorful presentation

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player can choose which rides to attempt in any order within their ticket budget constraints; no difficulty selection mentioned. Gameplay described as 'unforgiving' and 'deceptively challenging'

progression.upgrade_system: Ticket accumulation system - players earn points on rides that convert to tickets, which are spent to unlock access to new rides and eventually the Funhouse finale
progression.unlock_mechanism: Ticket currency gating; five themed amusement park rides each cost a certain number of tickets; sufficient accumulated tickets unlock Funhouse entrance
replayability_hooks: Multiple character abilities encourage revisiting rides with different mechanics
Point-scoring mechanics suggest optimization potential across different rides
Hidden level select available via Game Genie codes in Japanese version

world_building_techniques: Amusement park setting (Montana Land/Wackyland) provides thematic coherence
Character-specific ride themes reflect personality (Plucky bumper car, Hamton train, Babs rollercoaster, Furrball log ride, Buster funhouse)
Villain interference from Roderick Rat and antagonists; Montana Max reveal as final boss 'Secret Admirer'
Cameo appearances by additional Tiny Toons characters throughout
License IP integration - leverages television show character recognition and relationships

boss_design.philosophy: Single final confrontation with Montana Max as the 'Secret Admirer' antagonist; preceded by navigating Funhouse obstacles
notable_bosses: Montana Max (final boss, revealed as 'Secret Admirer' at Funhouse)

faction_indicators: Montana Max as wealthy antagonist constructing amusement park
Roderick Rat and villainous characters opposing player progression
Tiny Toons protagonist group (Buster, Babs, Plucky, Hamton, Furrball) as playable faction
danger_signals: Montana Max's trap-laden amusement park serves as false hospitality
Secret admirer mystery narrative creates false safety
Sweetie as antagonist on Furrball's ride (predator/prey dynamic from show)
environmental_storytelling: Five distinct amusement park ride environments reflect character personalities
Funhouse as final gauntlet of escalating obstacles
Ticket economy makes park access a gated, earned privilege
Regional naming difference (Montana Land vs. Wackyland) reflects setting ownership stakes

sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor (development history, unused assets, level select, regional differences)
wikipedia - Tiny Toon Adventures 2 article (gameplay mechanics, character ride assignments, narrative setup, reception analysis)
